title: Race-lap pit-window and traffic context features
summary: F1 pit decisions respond to the field, not only the driver's tyre wear: traffic, nearby rivals, undercut windows, and safety-car/VSC discounts change optimal stop timing.
rationale: F1 pit decisions respond to the field, not only the driver's tyre wear: traffic, nearby rivals, undercut windows, and safety-car/VSC discounts change optimal stop timing. The available tabular data can approximate this through same-race same-lap cohort features without using the target.
implementation_hint: For each Race-LapNumber cohort, add tyre-life rank/percentile, position rank vs tyre age, count/share of drivers with PitStop on current lap, previous-lap pit count by Race, median tyre life by position band, and gaps in Position/TyreLife to cars immediately ahead/behind. Preserve row order with groupby-transform and use only current or previous lap aggregates.
expected_effect: May improve detection of coordinated pit phases and undercut responses, especially rows where individual degradation alone is ambiguous.
risk: Current-lap cohort features may be transductive; validate with race-group folds to ensure they are allowed and stable.
